title:
Lesson 1:
It's Over / I Just Arrived
description: 'Se acabó' = It's over / It's finished / All gone. 'Acabo de llegar' = I just arrived. 'Tengo una cita' = I have an appointment (or a date).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Did the movie already end?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: Sí, se acabó hace diez minutos.
dialogue.english: Yes, it ended ten minutes ago.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: I just arrived! I missed it.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Why did you take so long?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.spanish: Tenía una cita con el doctor. Se fue tarde.
dialogue.english: I had a doctor's appointment. It ran late.

vocabulary.english: it's over / it's done / all gone
I have an appointment / a date

title:
Lesson 2:
Trendy / Family Introductions
description: 'Está de moda' = It's trendy / in fashion. For family: 'Es mi mamá' = This is my mom, 'Es mi papá' = my dad, 'Es mi hijo/hija' = my son/daughter, 'Es mi tío/tía' = my uncle/aunt.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: What a beautiful bag! Is it new?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: Sí, está de moda ahora. La vi en una revista.
dialogue.english: Yes, it's trendy now. I saw it in a magazine.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Hi! Who is she?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: Es mi mamá. Y él es mi tío.
dialogue.english: This is my mom. And he's my uncle.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Nice to meet you. And the child?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: Es mi hijo. Tiene cuatro años.
dialogue.english: He's my son. He's four years old.

title:
Lesson 3:
More or Less / Almost / Nothing
description: 'Más o menos' = more or less / so-so. 'Nada' = nothing / not at all. 'Casi' = almost. 'Tienes que probarlo' = you have to try it. These useful words fill many everyday situations.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: How was the trip?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: So-so. There was traffic.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Did you arrive on time?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Almost. I arrived five minutes late.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: And did you eat anything?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: Nada. Pero encontré un restaurante nuevo. ¡Tienes que probarlo!
dialogue.english: Nothing. But I found a new restaurant. You have to try it!

title:
Lesson 4:
For / Against / Think About It
description: 'Estoy a favor' = I'm in favor / I support it. 'Estoy en contra' = I'm against it. 'Piénsalo' = Think about it / Consider it. 'Todavía tienes tiempo' = You still have time.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: What do you think about the new project?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: Estoy a favor. Creo que puede funcionar.
dialogue.english: I'm in favor. I think it can work.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.spanish: Yo estoy en contra. Es muy caro.
dialogue.english: I'm against it. It's too expensive.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Think about it carefully before deciding.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.spanish: Todavía tienes tiempo para cambiar de opinión.
dialogue.english: You still have time to change your mind.

vocabulary.english: I'm in favor / I support it
